content.full_text:
RIVERSIDE COUNTY
DEMOCRATIC
CENTRAL COMMITTEE
Chair, Fundraising Committee
President: Tom Jameson
vice Chair East: Ray Strait
Vice Chair West: Dave Lanham
Secretary:
Treasurer: Betty Palmer
Parlimentarian: Sonja Wilson
7141789-8877
619/340-6655
Ju,,
January 16, l99l
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95 814
To Whom It May Concern:
The Riverside County Democratic Central Committee is undertaking a fundraising
campaign during 1991. The goal of the campaign is to raise S100,000.
As the chair of the Fundraising campaign, I have indicated that I would likc to establish
a separate organization to collect and hold the contributions. The funds raised will be
used to support "generic" party activities and will not be used to support candidates or
issues.
In order to accomplish this, and not be separated from the Central Committee, can the
Fundraising Committee prepare a separate Statement 410 with a different Treasurer,
submit it to the central committee and have them file the information with the FPPC?
This Committee would collect the funds and deposit them in a separate interest bearinB
account. When the Central Committee identified programs which required funding, funds
from this Committee would be paid out to the Central Committee for disbursement.
I would expect that our FPPC reports would be prepared and submitted to the Central
Committee for inclusion in their FPPC reports to you. The Fundraising Committee would
not file separately with FPPC.
Could you review my questions and respond in writing as to whether this makes sense in
terms of reporting our fundraising activity? Our Central Committee is meeting on
Sunday, February 3 and, if possible, I would appreciate your response by then. Il a fax
of your response is possible, I would appreciate the timeliness o[ this ellort very much.
My fax number is 619 /346-2539. (Please call first so I can plug it in.)
If none of the foregoing makes sense or you need clarification, please contact me at
619/340-6655. Thanks so much for your help.
Sincerely,
t,Ulte-+4%l/
Rena Fugate Y
P.O. Box 76
Palm Desert, CA 92260
l. -: ., '31

sections.question: In order to accomplish this, and not be separated from the Central Committee, can the Fundraising Committee prepare a separate Statement 410 with a different Treasurer, submit it to the central committee and have them file the information with the FPPC? This Committee would collect the funds and deposit them in a separate interest bearing account. When the Central Committee identified programs which required funding, funds from this Committee would be paid out to the Central Committee for disbursement. I would expect that our FPPC reports would be prepared and submitted to the Central Committee for inclusion in their FPPC reports to you. The Fundraising Committee would not file separately with FPPC. Could you review my questions and respond in writing as to whether this makes sense in terms of reporting our fundraising activity?
sections.question_synthetic: Can the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee establish a separate fundraising committee with its own Statement 410 and treasurer that collects contributions in a separate account and reports through the Central Committee's FPPC filings rather than filing separately?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a local Democratic committee to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The request seeks written guidance by February 3, 1991, and includes contact information and fax number for response.

embedding.summary: Rena Fugate, chair of the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee's fundraising campaign, requests FPPC guidance on whether a separate fundraising committee can file a Statement 410 with a different treasurer and submit reports through the Central Committee rather than filing directly with FPPC.
